message.content:
You are confirmer #2, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it; for a code change the named regression test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if the fix were reverted). Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix G-016 in .github/dependabot.yml + scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh
What: documented + scripted the lockfile regen Dependabot can't do (an auto-commit workflow was rejected for token/security reasons).
Test(s): test_dependabot_lockfile_regen_is_documented
Steps: read the actual changed file(s); read the test and judge whether it discriminates; check no regression. For a doc/refactor item, judge correctness/coverage rather than revert-discrimination. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested). Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
7dccfd8 fix(hardening-p4): batch E — egress, error contracts, PII & test discrimination (8 findings)
c49261e fix(hardening-p4): batch D — CI / supply-chain reproducibility (G-015,016,022)
2eea958 fix(hardening-p4): batch C — provenance, observability & resample memory (G-009,010,012,023)
91dfb1a fix(hardening-p4): batch B — concurrency, reasoning-tier & serialization (G-002,003,004)
d61bd4e fix(hardening-p4): batch A — methodology integrity (G-001,005,006,011,017)
0788961 docs(hardening): pass-3 merged (#161); open pass-4 with retrospective dimensions
54cd88c Merge pull request #161 from [REDACTED]/harden/pass-3
5f16960 docs(hardening): record pass-3 (49/54 confirmed, all fixed/reclassified) + pass-4 dimensions
1eb2f58 fix(hardening-p3): remediate 6 confirmation CONCERNs (2-agent pass)
ba82429 fix(hardening-p3): batch 6 — two-sided docs-drift guards (F-038)
---

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Regenerate the hash-pinned lockfiles from pyproject.toml (#G-016).
#
# Dependabot bumps the version ranges in pyproject.toml but cannot regenerate the uv lockfiles,
# so a Dependabot PR will fail the CI "requirements.lock is stale" gate until the lockfiles are
# regenerated. Run this on a Dependabot branch (or any PR that changes pyproject dependencies),
# then commit the updated requirements*.lock files:
#
#     scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh && git commit -am "chore: regenerate lockfiles"
#
# uv is pinned to the same version CI uses (#F-031) so the output matches the sync check exactly.
set -euo pipefail

UV_VERSION="0.11.21"

if ! command -v uv >/dev/null 2>&1 || [ "$(uv --version | awk '{print $2}')" != "$UV_VERSION" ]; then
  echo "installing uv==$UV_VERSION (CI-pinned) ..." >&2
  python -m pip install "uv==$UV_VERSION" >&2
fi

uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock
uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock

echo "regenerated requirements.lock + requirements-core.lock — review and commit them."

# Automated dependency + transitive-update monitoring (#147). Dependabot opens PRs for
# vulnerable/outdated dependencies on a weekly cadence so the supply-chain surface is tracked,
# not just scanned once. Pairs with the pip-audit gate (reproducible scan of requirements.lock)
# and the CycloneDX SBOM artifact in ci.yml.
#
# NOTE (#G-016): Dependabot bumps the ranges in pyproject.toml but does NOT regenerate the
# hash-pinned uv lockfiles (requirements.lock / requirements-core.lock), so a Dependabot PR will
# FAIL the CI "requirements.lock is stale" gate until a maintainer regenerates them. On a
# Dependabot PR, run `scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh` and commit the result to make it mergeable.
version: 2
updates:
  # Python packages declared in pyproject.toml (and resolved into requirements.lock).
  - package-ecosystem: pip
    directory: "/"
    schedule:
      interval: weekly
    open-pull-requests-limit: 10
    groups:
      # batch low-risk updates so the queue stays reviewable
      minor-and-patch:
        update-types: ["minor", "patch"]
  # The GitHub Actions used by the CI workflows themselves.
  - package-ecosystem: github-actions
    directory: "/"
    schedule:
      interval: weekly

transcripts/sessions/8d54714a-d616-4911-a386-9b4b0f2ce74d.jsonl:9862:{"parentUuid":"62ffb5e5-4ca5-404d-849c-6e78c907f64b","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_014ivL96NbugEgo2WBsoQqNa","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01EoegULFDpG1F91MNbk4zqB","name":"Edit","input":{"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_supply_chain.py","old_string":"def test_github_actions_are_sha_pinned():","new_string":"def test_sbom_step_applies_the_allowlist():
    # #G-015: the SBOM pip-audit (which exits non-zero on ANY finding) must apply the same
    # allowlist as the gate, or an ACCEPTED advisory fails SBOM generation under set -euo pipefail
    # even though the gate passed.
    text = _ci_text()
    sbom = text[text.index("SBOM (CycloneDX)") : text.index("Upload SBOM artifact")]
    assert "--ignore-vuln" in sbom and ".pip-audit-ignore" in sbom, (
        "SBOM step does not apply the allowlist (#G-015)"
    )
    assert 'cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}"' in sbom


def test_audit_tooling_is_version_pinned():
    # #G-022: pip-audit and pip-licenses must be pinned so the supply-chain gate is reproducible
    # run-to-run (a tooling bump can't silently change advisory/license results).
    text = _ci_text()
    assert "pip-audit==" in text, "pip-audit not version-pinned (#G-022)"
    assert "pip-licenses==" in text, "pip-licenses not version-pinned (#G-022)"
    assert "install --upgrade pip pip-audit\n" not in text, "unpinned pip-audit install reintroduced"


def test_dependabot_lockfile_regen_is_documented():
    # #G-016: Dependabot can't regenerate the uv lockfiles, so the manual regen path must be
    # documented + scripted, or every Dependabot PR is stuck on the stale-lock gate.
    regen = _ROOT / "scripts" / "regenerate_lockfiles.sh"
    assert regen.exists(), "no scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh (#G-016)"
    body = regen.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "uv pip compile" in body and "requirements-core.lock" in body
    db = (_ROOT / ".github" / "dependabot.yml").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "regenerate_lockfiles.sh" in db, "dependabot.yml does not point at the regen script"

cd /tmp && rm -rf g016test && cp -r /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay g016test 2>/dev/null; cd /tmp/g016test && git stash -u >/dev/null 2>&1

rm scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh
.venv/bin/pytest tests/test_supply_chain.py::test_dependabot_lockfile_regen_is_documented -q 2>&1 | tail -4
echo "=== restore script, revert dependabot.yml reference ==="

# remove the regen reference line from dependabot.yml
grep -v "regenerate_lockfiles.sh" .github/dependabot.yml > /tmp/db.tmp && mv /tmp/db.tmp .github/dependabot.yml

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && echo "=== CI lock generation/check commands ===" && grep -n "pip compile\|requirements.lock\|requirements-core.lock\|all-extras\|generate-hashes\|--extra dev\|universal" .github/workflows/ci.yml
input.description: Compare CI compile commands to script

content.content:
=== CI lock generation/check commands ===
28:          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock
69:          # Install from the hashed all-extras lockfile so the integration suite executes against
73:          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
86:  # (requirements.lock) so the audit is reproducible across runs and matches what installs
108:          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements.regen.lock
110:          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements.regen.lock) \
111:            || { echo "::error::requirements.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock"; exit 1; }
114:          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
115:          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
116:            || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock"; exit 1; }
131:          pip-audit --strict --require-hashes --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock "${IGNORES[@]}"
151:          pip-audit --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock --format cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}"
168:          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock

content.text:
The script's compile commands match CI's staleness-check commands exactly:
- `requirements.lock`: `uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes` (CI line 108 ↔ script)
- `requirements-core.lock`: `uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes` (CI line 114 ↔ script)

And the uv version is pinned to `0.11.21`, matching CI line 107. So running the script produces output that will actually pass the CI gate.

Assessment complete.

Findings:
- **Appropriate**: Correct, idiomatic fix. The doc-comment in `.github/dependabot.yml` explains the limitation and points maintainers at `scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh`. The script (`set -euo pipefail`, executable bit set, uv pinned to the CI version `0.11.21`) runs the exact same `uv pip compile` invocations CI uses to check staleness (both extras variants, `--universal --generate-hashes`), so its output will actually satisfy the gate. No regression — only additive comment lines + new script.
- **Complete / discriminating**: Test `test_dependabot_lockfile_regen_is_documented` (tests/test_supply_chain.py:266) passes now and FAILS on revert of *either* side — removing the script fails at line 270, removing the dependabot.yml reference fails at line 274. The `uv pip compile` and `requirements-core.lock` substring assertions guard against a stub script.

Minor (non-blocking) note: the test checks for presence of substrings rather than the correctness of the compile flags, so it wouldn't catch a future drift between the script's flags and CI's flags. But the auto-commit-rejection rationale is a doc-only design note (not testable), and the named test discriminates on the documented+scripted requirement as specified.
